message:
feat(ipc): Cx-first IpcServer::bind_with_cx + bind_with_permissions_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Add Cx-first siblings for the two IPC server bind entry points on
the unix IpcServer impl. These are called from CLI startup and are
the first async surface the daemon touches after argument parsing.

1. `IpcServer::bind_with_cx(cx, socket_path)` — pre-flight delegate
   that routes through `bind_with_permissions_with_cx` with the
   default 0o600 permissions.

2. `IpcServer::bind_with_permissions_with_cx(cx, socket_path,
   permissions)` — 4-seam checkpoint structure:
   - Pre-flight (before any filesystem mutation)
   - Before stale-socket removal
   - Before parent-dir creation
   - Before listener bind
   Responsive cancellation at each syscall boundary — a cx-driven
   CLI cancelled mid-startup won't touch files it doesn't need to.

Legacy `bind` + `bind_with_permissions` preserved unchanged.

Two regression tests pin the contract:
- `ipc_server_bind_with_cx_matches_legacy` — fresh cx produces
  equivalent server (same socket_path, socket file exists).
- `ipc_server_bind_with_precancelled_cx_fails_before_filesystem_mutation`
  — pre-cancelled cx fails with Interrupted error + "cancelled"
  in message + socket must NOT exist after.

Verified via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/
ft-rusticmaple-target, --features asupersync-runtime): 97/97 ipc
tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
